  Source   Edit

boring utilities likely useful to multiple pieces of cps machinery

Types

NodeFilter = proc (n: NimNode): NimNode
  Source   Edit 
Continuation = concept c
    c.fn is ContinuationProc[Continuation]
    c is ref object
    c of RootObj
  Source   Edit 
ContinuationProc[T] = proc (c: T): T {...}{.nimcall.}
  Source   Edit 
Pair = tuple[key: NimNode, val: NimNode]
  Source   Edit

Consts

cpsDebug = false
produce gratuitous output   Source   Edit 
cpsMagicExists = true
make use of .cpsMagic.   Source   Edit 
cpsMutant = false
mutate continuations   Source   Edit 
cpsMoves = false
try to =move versus =copy   Source   Edit 
cpsCast = false
use cast instead of conversion   Source   Edit 
cpsTrace = false
store "stack" traces   Source   Edit 
cpsExcept = false
also stash exceptions   Source   Edit 
cpsFn = false
multiple fns in continuations   Source   Edit 
cpsTrampBooty = false
put a tramp in da booty   Source   Edit 
comments = false
embed comments within the transformation   Source   Edit

Procs

func isEmpty(n: NimNode): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
true if the node n is Empty   Source   Edit 
proc filter(n: NimNode; f: NodeFilter): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
  Source   Edit 
proc desym(n: NimNode): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
  Source   Edit 
proc unhide(n: NimNode): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
unwrap hidden conversion nodes and erase their types   Source   Edit 
proc resym(n: NimNode; sym: NimNode; field: NimNode): NimNode {...}{.raises: [],
    tags: [].}
seems we only use this for rewriting local symbols into symbols in the env, so we'll do custom handling of identDefs here also   Source   Edit 
proc replacedSymsWithIdents(n: NimNode): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
  Source   Edit 
func stripComments(n: NimNode): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
remove doc statements because that was a stupid idea   Source   Edit 
proc hasPragma(n: NimNode; s: static[string]): bool
true if the n holds the pragma s   Source   Edit 
proc filterPragma(ns: seq[NimNode]; liftee: NimNode): NimNode {...}{.raises: [],
    tags: [].}
given a seq of pragmas, omit a match and return Pragma or Empty   Source   Edit 
proc stripPragma(n: NimNode; s: static[string]): NimNode
filter a pragma with the matching name from various nodes   Source   Edit 
func doc(s: string): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
generate a doc statement for debugging   Source   Edit 
proc doc(n: var NimNode; s: string) {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
add a doc statement to the ast for debugging   Source   Edit 
proc hash(n: NimNode): Hash {...}{.raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect].}
  Source   Edit 
proc init[T](c: T; l: LineInfo): T
  Source   Edit 
proc definedName(n: NimNode): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
create an identifier from an typesection/identDef as cached; this is a copy and it is repr'd to ensure gensym compat...   Source   Edit 
proc stripVar(n: NimNode): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
pull the type out of a VarTy   Source   Edit 
proc letOrVar(n: NimNode): NimNodeKind {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
choose between let or var for proc parameters   Source   Edit 
proc isLiftable(n: NimNode): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
is this a node we should float to top-level?   Source   Edit 
proc hasLiftableChild(n: NimNode): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
  Source   Edit 
proc errorAst(s: string): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
produce {.error: s.} in order to embed errors in the ast   Source   Edit 
proc errorAst(n: NimNode; s = "creepy ast"): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
embed an error with a message   Source   Edit

Templates

template cpsLift() {...}{.pragma.}
lift this proc|type   Source   Edit 
template cpsCall() {...}{.pragma.}

template cpsCall(n: typed) {...}{.pragma.}
redirection   Source   Edit
